rationale: This page contains a detailed summary of the Digital Personal Data Protection (DPDP) Act, 2023, including its objectives, key provisions, and penalties. It is a core topic for Polity and Governance and provides high-quality factual content for question generation.
cleanedText:
7. IMPORTANT LEGISLATIONS/BILLS 7.1. DIGITAL PERSONAL DATA PROTECTION (DPDP) ACT, 2023 Why in the news? Recently, Union Government has notified large parts of the Digital Personal Data Protection (DPDP) Act, 2023. It has also notified Digital Personal Data Protection (DPDP) Rules, 2025. About Digital Personal Data Protection (DPDP) Act 2023 - It aims to regulate the processing of digital personal data while ensuring individuals' right to protect their data. - It protects digital personal data by providing: - Obligations of Data Fiduciaries (persons, companies and government entities who process data) for data processing (that is, collection, storage or any other operation on personal data); - The rights and duties of Data Principals (that is, the person to whom the data relates); - Financial penalties for breach of rights, duties, and obligations. Key Provisions of the Act | Specification | Key Details | | :--- | :--- | | Applicability | - Applies to digital personal data processed in India (digital or digitised).
- Applies to processing outside India if offering goods/services in India.
- Excludes personal-purpose data and data made publicly available by the Data Principal or under legal obligation. | | Consent | - Data processed only for lawful purpose with consent, withdrawable anytime.
- Consent not required for legitimate uses (government benefits, medical emergencies, etc.).
- For children or persons with disability, consent by parent/legal guardian. | | Data Protection Board of India (DPBI) | - Established by Central Government.
- Functions: monitor compliance, impose penalties, order remedial measures, hear grievances.
- Members appointed for 2 years, eligible for reappointment.
- Appeals lie with TDSAT. | | Rights & Duties of Data Principal | - Rights: access information, correction & erasure, grievance redressal, nomination of representative.
- Duty: no false/frivolous complaints or false particulars.
- Penalty for violation: up to ₹10,000. | | Obligations of Data Fiduciaries | - Ensure accuracy and completeness of data.
- Implement reasonable security safeguards.
- Inform DPBI and affected persons in case of breach.
- Erase data once purpose is met unless legally required. | | Significant Data Fiduciaries (SDF) | - Notified by Centre based on data volume/sensitivity, risks to rights, sovereignty, security, electoral democracy, public order.
- Additional duties: Data Protection Officer, independent auditor, impact assessments. | | Children’s Data Protection | - Prohibits processing harmful to child’s well-being.
- No tracking, behavioural monitoring, or targeted advertising for children. | | Cross-border Data Transfer | - Permitted except to countries restricted by the government. | | Penalties | - Up to ₹200 crore for child-related violations.
- Up to ₹250 crore for failure to prevent data breaches. |
